chapter_title.ar: باب اللِّبَاسِ الَّذِي تُكْرَهُ الصَّلَاةُ فِيهِ وَ مَا لَا تُكْرَهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 60 - The clothes in which the Salāt is disliked, and what is not disliked

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/320)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/320)
text: هُ سَأَلَهُ عَنْ جُلُودِ الدَّارِشِ الَّتِي يُتَّخَذُ مِنْهَا الْخِفَافُ قَالَ فَقَالَ لَا تُصَلِّ فِيهَا فَإِنَّهَا تُدْبَغُ بِخُرْءِ الْكِلَابِ.

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Idrees, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from Al Sayyari, from Abu Yazeed Al Qasmy, and Qasam, it is a village on the way from Al Yemen with Al Basra,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan Al-Rezaasws, having been asked about the skins of ‘Al-Darish’ from which shoes are taken to be. So heasws said: ‘You cannot pray Salāt in it, for it is tanned with the excretion of dogs’.
en.sarwar: 25. Ahmad ibn Idris has narrated from Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Sayyariy from abu Yazid al-Qasmiy, Qasmah is a village in Yeman in Basra, who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about al-Darish skin from which slippers are made. He (the Imam) said, ‘Do not perform Salah (prayer) with them because it is tanned with the leftover of dogs.’”
